They see themselves strictly as a product and tech company. Their goal is to democratize commerce and finance. This tech-first mindset is critical. They treat regional infrastructure bottlenecks purely as engineering problems.

Szarfsztejn joined the company in 2017. He started the logistics division from scratch. He grew the team from 60 people to over 70k employees in just 6 years. This completely changed ecom in Latin America. Delivery times dropped from 6 days to same-day delivery. They hire based on cultural fit and adaptability. 65% of warehouse workers get their first job there. 55% of these workers are women.

AI is a core operational focus. They use AI to create extreme workforce efficiency. Customer support cases have grown massively. However, they use fewer support agents today because AI handles the chats and calls. Developers rely heavily on AI tools. Over 20% of all new code at the company is generated by AI. They also use AI to improve search algorithms, product pages, and user reviews.

Financial inclusion is their biggest future opportunity. Traditional banks ignore massive populations in Latin America. Mercado Libre disrupted this system. They launched accounts that pay interest on user deposits. In Argentina alone, 18M people now invest their money through the platform. They also use data and algorithms to lend money. They provide credit to millions of small businesses and unbanked individuals.

Most importantly, competition only makes the company stronger. Global giants like $AMZN and new Asian platforms force them to innovate faster. They will continue to prioritize execution and risk-taking. They will ignore distractions and focus strictly on scaling commerce and fintech.
